//repos_note: The repos to discover candidate PRs in. Blank until you configure it: agent-code-review repos add <owner/name> [--allowed-authors-only]. Repos listed in allowed_authors_only_repos only discover PRs from allowed authors; others discover any open PR.
//authors: Allowed authors (whose PRs we may approve) live in the DuckDB store, per repo. Manage with: agent-code-review authors allow <owner/repo|*> <github-handle> [--name ... --email ... --slack-id ...]
//candidates_note: rereview_cooldown holds a PR after one of our own reviews before it can be re-discovered; quiet_period holds a PR until it has gone untouched that long (no pushes or edits). Held PRs sit in the queue as not-yet-eligible; promotion or a manual add bypasses both. '0s' disables a hold.
//schedule_note: Two independent loops, each with its own switch: discovery scrapes repos for candidates (deterministic gh calls, no LLM); schedule runs review cycles (codex invocations, hence max_parallel). The eligibility holds keep a tight review interval cheap: idle cycles are no-ops.

schedule.//usage_floor_note: Pause the review loop when a Codex window has less than this % remaining; reviews resume when the window refills. 0 disables a floor. Omit for the default of 10.

review.//main_prompt_note: The tool assumes only the gh and codex CLIs. Your prompt may direct the agent to use anything else you have set up (skills such as pr-issue-review, extra CLIs such as agent-* tools); that is your prompt's business, and you grant any extra access via codex.args.
review.main_prompt: Act as a friendly PR unblocker. Review this pull request thoroughly: read the diff, the PR description, and any linked context via the gh CLI. Leave one GitHub review with inline comments that help the author move forward.
review.//post_outcome_note: on_approve / on_comment / on_reject run after the agent lands on that outcome (reject = requested changes). Put your workspace-specific conventions here (team channels, emoji rituals, notification tooling); the tool ships none of that.
review.//rules_note: Comment-only vs approval is a built-in, always-present directive (default: comment-only; approval only when the author is on the allowed-authors list for this repo and it isn't self-authored); you do not need a rule for it. Rules add EXTRA instructions when their condition matches (author_allowed / author_not_allowed / author_is_gh_user / candidate_type / repos). Give a rule an "outcome" (approve|comment|reject) to attach it under that post-outcome section instead of the prompt body, so behaviour can branch on the allow-list deterministically. Manage rules with the 'prompts rules' command (agent-code-review prompts rules usage).
review.//resume_prompt_note: When a codex run ends on an intermediate WORKING report (the agent yielded its turn before finishing), the driver resumes the session with this nudge instead of recording an ERROR. Empty uses a built-in default; codex.max_resumes bounds the attempts.

codex.//model_note: Leave empty to use codex's own default model. To pin one, pick a valid id from `codex debug models`; an invalid id fails every review with a 400.
codex.//effort_note: Leave empty to use the selected model's default reasoning effort. To pin one, use an effort the selected model supports (today commonly low, medium, high, or xhigh).
codex.//args_note: workspace-write disables network by default, but the agent must reach GitHub via gh; this override re-enables network while keeping writes scoped to the per-PR workdir.
codex.//max_resumes_note: How many times to resume a session that ended on a WORKING report before recording an ERROR. Unset means 2; 0 disables resuming.
